title: Decision: Use fn pointers (not Fn closures) in registration structs to satisfy Sync+Static
description: BackendRegistration, WritableBackendRegistration, and SigningBackendRegistration all use fn pointers for the factory field (e.g. line 1383: 'pub factory: fn(&str) -> Result<...>'). Function pointers are Sync and 'static by default, enabling the registration structs themselves to be Sync and stored in the inventory global collection. Fn closures with captured environments would require Sync bound on the captured type, complicating registration. The factory is a stateless dispatch function, making the fn pointer the simplest and most correct choice. This avoids closures entirely, eliminating a source of lifetime and trait bound complexity.
